name: Broiled Chicken with Thyme, Fennel, and Peppers (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 2+3/4 pounds total -- trimmed and quartered lengthwise
3 red bell peppers, cut into 1-inch-wide strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook fennel bulbs in a large saucepan of boiling salted water until crisp-tender, about 8 minutes. Drain and cool.
2. Toss fennel, bell peppers, olive oil, and 1 teaspoon chopped thyme in a large bowl to coat. Season with salt and pepper (can be made 1 day ahead; cover and refrigerate).
3. Preheat the broiler.
4. Pound each chicken breast between sheets of plastic to 1/2-inch thickness using a mallet or rolling pin. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
5. Place chicken, skin side down, on the broiler pan.
6. Broil chicken 4 to 5 inches from heat source until golden brown and almost cooked through, about 5 minutes. Remove the broiler pan from the oven.
7. Pour off any pan drippings using tongs. Turn chicken over and broil until skin is golden brown and chicken is cooked through, about 4 more minutes.
8. Transfer chicken to a platter and tent with foil to keep warm. Pour off any additional pan drippings.
9. Working in batches, arrange peppers and fennel in a single layer on the broiler pan. Broil until vegetables begin to brown and are tender, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer vegetables to the platter with chicken.
10. Melt butter in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Mix in remaining 2 teaspoons chopped thyme, lemon peel, and fennel seeds. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Drizzle the butter mixture over the chicken. Garnish with thyme sprigs if desired, and serve. Makes 6 servings.
